content.problemStatement:
Chef has nnn cities, some of which are connected directly, while others are not. If city aaa is directly connected to city bbb, and city bbb is directly connected to city ccc, then city aaa is indirectly connected to city ccc. Chef wants to find the number of provinces, where a province is defined as a group of cities that are directly or indirectly connected to each other and not connected to any city outside the group.

Chef is given an n×nn \times nn×n connection matrix connectionMatrixconnectionMatrixconnectionMatrix where connectionMatrix[i][j]=1connectionMatrix[i][j] = 1connectionMatrix[i][j]=1 indicates that city iii is directly connected to city jjj, and `0` otherwise. Help Chef determine how many provinces exist among these cities.

Function DeclarationFunction NamecountProvincescountProvincescountProvinces

Parameters
connectionMatrixconnectionMatrixconnectionMatrix : A square matrix representing direct connections between cities, where connectionMatrix[i][j]connectionMatrix[i][j]connectionMatrix[i][j] is 111 if city iii is directly connected to city jjj, and 000 otherwise.
The size of the matrix is n×nn \times nn×n, where 1≤n≤2001 \leq n \leq 2001≤n≤200.
Return Value
Returns an integer representing the total number of provinces (connected components of cities).
Constraints
1≤n≤2001 \leq n \leq 2001≤n≤200
`connectionMatrix` is an n×nn \times nn×n matrix.
Each element of `connectionMatrix` is either 000 or 111.
For all iii, `connectionMatrix[i][i] = 1`.
`connectionMatrix[i][j] = connectionMatrix[j][i]` for all i,ji, ji,j (the matrix is symmetric).

Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT — the number of test cases.
For each test case:

The first line contains an integer nnn — the number of cities.
The next nnn lines each contain nnn integers representing the `connectionMatrix` for that test case.




Output Format

For each test case, print a single integer — the number of provinces Chef has identified.

Help Chef implement the `countProvinces` function to find the number of connected groups of cities!

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1

City 0 and 1 are connected -> one province
City 2 is isolated -> one province

Total provinces = 2
Test Case 2

All cities are connected directly or indirectly
They form one single group

Total provinces = 1
Test Case 3

Only one city
It forms a province by itself

Total provinces = 1

solution.timeComplexity: O(n²), because you check the connection between every pair of cities.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n) for the visited array and recursion stack.
